title: Prepositions
description: Use in, on, at, under, and other location and time linkers.

forms.positive: Preposition of Place (in, on, at, under, behind) | Preposition of Time (in, on, at, before, after) + Noun Phrase
forms.question: Does the preposition indicate time (when) or space/location (where)?

usages.description: Prepositions of place show the position of a noun relative to another entity. 'In' is used for enclosed spaces, 'on' for surfaces, and 'at' for specific points.
examples: The keys are **on** the dining table.
Meet me **at** the entrance of the mall.

usages.description: Prepositions of time specify when an action occurs. Use 'at' for clock times, 'on' for days/dates, and 'in' for months, years, and long periods.
examples: It gets very hot here **in** summer.

commonMistakes.incorrect: I will meet you in Monday.
commonMistakes.correct: I will meet you on Monday.
commonMistakes.explanation: Use 'on' for specific days of the week (Monday, Tuesday, etc.) or calendar dates, not 'in'.
For public transport vehicles where you can walk around (bus, train, plane, ship), we use 'on'. Use 'in' for private passenger cars, taxis, or small boats.

quizzes.question: Fill in the blank: The painting is hanging _______ the wall.
quizzes.explanation: The wall represents a vertical surface, so we use 'on'.

quizzes.question: Fill in the blank: The concert starts _______ 7:30 PM.
quizzes.explanation: We use 'at' for specific clock times.

quizzes.question: Choose the correct preposition: She was born _______ 2005.
